items.title: Chiral supramolecular alloys: programmable 2D-3D control via Co-assembly of isomorphous β-peptide foldamers
items.description: We report the co-assembly of isomorphous yet chemically distinct β-peptide foldamers, creating chiral supramolecular alloys. This system enables composition-dependent 2D-3D control, programmably steering the morphology from 3D molar-tooth to 2D square-plate architectures. This alloy-like blending principle provides a general strategy for the programmable design of foldamer-based supramolecular systems.

items.description: BACKGROUND: Few studies have examined how empathy develops in neonatal intensive care units (NICUs) as nurses experience negative outcomes in end-of-life care delivery (eg, burnout, moral conflict, and psychological distress). items.description: CONCLUSION: CCG reconstruction provides clinically meaningful and durable improvement in mouth opening that is retained for up to 3 decades. Findings support continued use of CCG when biologic reconstruction is preferred, but mandate structured long-term surveillance until growth completion.
